# Verify that `sesam init --sesam-dir <subdir>` writes git-config driver
# entries that carry the `--sesam-dir=<subdir>` flag. Git invokes those
# drivers/aliases with cwd = worktree root, so without the flag they would
# fail to find the nested `.sesam/` tree.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

mkdir sub
exec sesam init --user admin --sesam-dir sub

# The installed driver/alias entries in .git/config must carry --sesam-dir=sub
# so the binary resolves the nested .sesam/ when git invokes it from the
# worktree root.
exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv
stdout '^sesam --sesam-dir=sub show$'

exec git config --local alias.sesam
stdout '^!sesam --sesam-dir=sub$'

exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# End-to-end: git runs the alias (`!sesam --sesam-dir=sub`) as a shell command
# with cwd = worktree root. The baked-in flag must let it resolve the nested
# repo and list its secrets - here the README init created under sub/.
exec git sesam ls
stdout 'README\.md'
stdout 'admin'
